titleStmt.title: Letter from Peter Mooney to his sister Katie Mooney, 29 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Peter Mooney

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
Letter from Peter Mooney to his sister telling her that the reason he hasn't been writing is because he is away in France on a march. He tells her that anything sent to him is surely lost. He hopes he won't be long there as he has no money and sleeping is hard yet the weather is lovely and there is plenty to eat. He wrote a letter previous to Paddy, telling how he hopes Sinn Féin burn to the ground.
He writes an address on the back an address if she wishes to send him anything.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
May 29th 1916 Dear Katie I am sure you are expecting a letter from
me this long time. I had not the time for writing
as we were three days on the march + a weary time
it was too. I hope you did not send me any parcels
lately if you did they will get lost. At present I
am attached to no division so do not write until you
hear from me again. We are having a rest just now
but I would rather be attached to some Division as time
hangs very heavy on me & I have no money I hope we
will not be here long. I keep in the very best of health
& we are getting plenty to eat & the weather is lovely
& fine. I wrote to Paddy first before we started on
the march I hope he received it alright. I hope that
Sinn Fein Crowd are entirely wiped out of Ireland
now. They know by this time the strength of England &
so will the Huns in a short time we are the boys
to make things burn when we start. I hope all
are well in the Paddock remember me to them all.
I think I could sleep out in the garden at home now
I have slept in a lot worse places out here. I have
no more to say at present I will write soon again.
I now say good. Bye & may God - Bless & keep you all.
Good Bye. Peter Mooney
you might send me a Postal Order
for 6/- as I might be here for some
time register it I may get it
sometime. My address:
Dr. N. Mooney 101267: R. F.A.
No 6 Large Rest Camp
B. E. F France.
Miss K Mooney
Caulstown
Dunboyne
Co Meath
Ireland
Army Post Office 29 My 16
